heading: 9.7.14.6.Matrix multiply-accumulate operation usingmma.spinstruction with sparse matrix A (part 4)
instruction: Matrix multiply-accumulate operation usingmma.spinstruction with sparse matrix A
content:
| .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u8` / `.s8` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, each containing four `.u8` / `.s8` elements from matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3, …, b15 |
  | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, each containing four `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` elements from matrix B. |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 132](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b1),
  [Figure 133](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b2), [Figure 134](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b3) and [Figure 135](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b4).

Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=64

GRID STRUCTURE OVERVIEW:
- Rows (matrix rows covered): 0-15, divided into 4 bands of 4 rows each
- Columns (register groups): 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 column groups total)
- Each thread holds 4 register elements: b0, b1, b2, b3
- Thread indexing label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row band 0 (matrix rows 0-3):
  Column 0: T0  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T4  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied, shown with arrow continuation)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row band 1 (matrix rows 4-7):
  Column 0: T1  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T5  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T29 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row band 2 (matrix rows 8-11):
  Column 0: T2  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T6  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T30 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row band 3 (matrix rows 12-15):
  Column 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0-T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
- Threads T8-T11 cover column 2 (implied), same row pattern
- Pattern continues: threads T(4*col + row_band) cover column col, row band row_band
- Threads T28-T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
- Total threads used: 32 (T0 through T31)
- Total columns: 8 (columns 0 through 7)
- 4 threads per column group, 8 column groups = 32 threads total

REGISTER PACKING:
- Each thread holds 4 register elements labeled b0, b1, b2, b3
- These are packed into 32-bit registers
- For 8-bit types (.u8/.s8/.e4m3/.e5m2/.e3m2/.e2m3/.e2m1): 4 elements packed per 32-bit register (b0, b1, b2, b3 packed into one 32-bit register word)
- Each thread thus uses 1 register of 32 bits containing 4 packed 8-bit (or sub-8-bit) values

SUMMARY:
- 32 threads (T0-T31) each hold 4 elements (b0-b3) packed in registers
- Thread Tn covers: row band = (n mod 4), column group = floor(n / 4)
- Column groups span columns 0-7 of the B matrix fragment
- Row bands span rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 of the B matrix fragment

T1     | 20-23       | 0      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T2     | 24-27       | 0      | b4, b5, b6, b7

PACKING DETAILS
Each thread holds 4 register elements: b4, b5, b6, b7
These are packed into 32-bit registers. For 8-bit types (u8/s8/e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1), four 8-bit values fit into one 32-bit register, so b4, b5, b6, b7 together occupy 2 registers (two 8-bit values packed per register, or all four in two 32-bit registers depending on packing).

PATTERN SUMMARY
- 4 row groups of 4 rows each span rows 16-31
- 8 column groups (columns 0-7) correspond to threads T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31
- Within each row-group/column intersection, one thread holds 4 elements (b4, b5, b6, b7)
- Thread numbering: T(row_group + 4*col_group) where row_group in {0,1,2,3} and col_group in {0,1,...,7}

=== THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN ===
Thread ID within each column group follows this pattern:
  Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (for row groups 32-35, 36-39, 40-43, 44-47 respectively)
  Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
  Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
  Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
  Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
  Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
  Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
  Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

General formula: Thread = (column_index * 4) + row_group_index
  where row_group_index: 0=rows32-35, 1=rows36-39, 2=rows40-43, 3=rows44-47

=== REGISTER ELEMENT DETAILS ===
Each thread stores 4 elements labeled b8, b9, b10, b11
These are the SECOND set of 4 register elements (b0-b7 appear in rows 0-31, b8-b11 appear in rows 32-47)
For 8-bit data types (u8/s8) or floating point types (e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1):
  Multiple 8-bit elements are packed into each 32-bit register
  b8 and b9 are packed into one 32-bit register
  b10 and b11 are packed into one 32-bit register
  (4 elements total per thread = 2 registers of 32 bits each, with 2 elements per register for 8-bit types)

=== TOTAL THREAD COVERAGE ===
All 32 threads (T0-T31) are used
Each thread covers one specific (row-group, column) intersection
32 threads x 4 elements = 128 element slots covering the 16-row x 8-column sparse B fragment for rows 32-47

- For 8-bit types (.u8/.s8) and floating point sub-byte types (.e4m3/.e5m2/.e3m2/.e2m3/.e2m1): multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN

Rows 0–7 use threads T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28 (stride of +4 per row).
  Row 0 -> T0
  Row 1 -> T4
  Row 2 -> T8
  Row 3 -> T12
  Row 4 -> T16
  Row 5 -> T20
  Row 6 -> T24
  Row 7 -> T28

Rows 8–15 use threads T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29 (stride of +4 per row, offset +1 from upper half).
  Row 8  -> T1
  Row 9  -> T5
  Row 10 -> T9
  Row 11 -> T13
  Row 12 -> T17
  Row 13 -> T21
  Row 14 -> T25
  Row 15 -> T29

BIT FIELD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN

Each thread holds a single 32-bit metadata register covering all 8 column groups (cols 0..31).
Each group of 4 columns consumes exactly 4 bits of the metadata register.
The bit fields are contiguous and non-overlapping across the 32-bit register:

  Cols 0..3:   bits [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   bits [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  bits [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: bits [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: bits [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: bits [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: bits [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: bits [31..28]

This means each 4-bit field encodes the sparsity selector for 4 consecutive columns within one matrix row. For 2:4 structured sparsity, each group of 4 elements contains exactly 2 non-zeros; 4 bits encode two 2-bit selectors (each 2-bit value selects which of the 4 positions is non-zero).

DIAGONAL ARROWS
Two diagonal arrows are shown in the figure:
1. An arrow from the row 1 / cols 28..31 cell (T4: [31..28]) pointing diagonally down-left toward row 7 / cols 0..3 (T28: [3..0]), indicating the continuation of the thread sequence T4 -> T8 -> ... -> T28 across rows 2–7.
2. An arrow from the row 9 / cols 28..31 cell (T5: [31..28]) pointing diagonally down-left toward row 15 / cols 0..3 (T29: [3..0]), indicating the continuation of the thread sequence T5 -> T9 -> ... -> T29 across rows 10–15.

SUMMARY OF KEY STRUCTURAL RULES
1. Each matrix row maps to exactly one thread.
2. Each thread's 32-bit register covers all 32 columns (cols 0..31) for that row.
3. Bit fields are 4 bits wide per 4-column group, packed LSB to MSB left to right.
4. Upper 8 rows (0–7) use even-indexed threads in multiples of 4: T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28.
5. Lower 8 rows (8–15) use odd-indexed threads offset by 1: T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29.
6. Threads T2, T3, T6, T7, T10, T11, etc. (those with index mod 4 = 2 or 3) are not used for this metadata layout in columns 0–31 (they likely cover columns 32–63, the second half of k=64).

Each thread covers 16 columns of K (one 16-column group) across 8 rows (either rows 0-7 or rows 8-15), with 8 four-bit elements packed per 32-bit register.

6. METADATA ENCODING FORMAT
=============================================================
For .u4/.s4 data type with k=64:
Each group of 8 columns contains 8 elements of 4-bit data.
With 2:4 sparsity, every 4 consecutive elements have 2 non-zeros.
A 4-bit metadata field encodes the positions of 2 non-zero elements
out of every 4 elements, using 2 bits per selected position (2 bits x 2 = 4 bits per group of 4 elements).

Within each 4-bit metadata field:
  Bits [1..0]: index (0,1,2,3) of the first non-zero element in the group of 4
  Bits [3..2]: index (0,1,2,3)
